architecture.summary: RAG-IoE integrates a context-aware data model for Internet of Everything (IoE) networks with a hybrid retrieval-augmented generation pipeline. The system organizes IoE data within a semantic framework, combining structured search on a Knowledge Graph with vector database retrieval of embeddings from unstructured data. Retrieved results are refined through LLM-driven reasoning, producing context-based information guidance for industrial workers. The seam is text: external structured and unstructured data sources are retrieved and formatted into text prompts for the LLM.

biblio.title: RAG-IoE: IoT context-aware information retrieval with Large Language Models in Industry 5.0
biblio.venue: ACM Transactions on Internet of Things

claims.statement: RAG-IoE combines structured search on a Knowledge Graph with unstructured data retrieval using embeddings stored in a vector database, followed by LLM-driven reasoning to refine results
The architecture enhances decision-making, reduces cognitive overload, and ensures precise guidance for industrial operators

The approach organizes IoE data within a semantic framework, integrating hybrid retrieval methods to enable adaptive, scalable, and context-based information retrieval from both structured and unstructured data sources
RAG-IoE addresses the challenge that traditional information retrieval techniques struggle with the heterogeneous, dynamic data generated in industrial settings
